issues.title: You should not need to provide an openai api key in application.properties if you set it manually via code every time you call it
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, the feature you are about to request might have already been requested.

**Expected Behavior**

I do not set the spring.ai.openai.api-key in my application.properties, if i call a chatmodel that contains a key i get no error, if i call a chatmodel that does not contain an api key a get an error

<!--- Tell us how it should work. Add a code example to explain what you think the feature should look like. This is optional, but it would help up understand your expectations. -->

**Current Behavior**

if i run my application without setting an spring.ai.openai.api-keyi get the error 
Error creating bean with name 'openAiChatModel' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/ai/model/openai/autoconfigure/OpenAiChatAutoConfiguration.class]: Failed to instantiate [org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel]: Factory method 'openAiChatModel' threw exception with message: OpenAI API key must be set. Use the connection property: spring.ai.openai.api-key or spring.ai.openai.chat.api-key property.



<!--- Explain the difference from current behavior and why do you need this feature (aka why it is not possible to implement the desired functionality with the current version) -->

**Context**

My application is distributed to clients who provide their own key in a separate configuration file that is read at runtime, without this feature i need to include a dummy key to make the application run 
Another satisfactory solution would be to create an application.property setting to start the application without the key

<!--- 
How has this issue affected you?
What are you trying to accomplish?
What other alternatives have you considered?
Are you aware of any workarounds?
-->

comments.body:
You can set the value to _ and the application should start. 

```spring.ai.openai.api-key=_```

This is not a bug, neither a feature request.

issues.title: JdbcChatMemoryRepositoryProperties注入失败
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, there might be already a duplicate issue for the one you are about to create.
If the bug is trivial, just go ahead and create the issue. Otherwise, please take a few moments and fill in the following sections:

**Bug description**
JdbcChatMemoryRepositorySchemaInitializer的bean依赖JdbcChatMemoryRepositoryProperties的默认地址`classpath:org/springframework/ai/chat/memory/repository/jdbc/schema-@@platform@@.sql`,无效，而且他只是貌似只是检查路径，并不执行

**Environment**
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>org.example</groupId>
    <artifactId>springai-test</artifactId>
    <version>1.0-SNAPSHOT</version>

    <properties>
        <maven.compiler.source>17</maven.compiler.source>
        <maven.compiler.target>17</maven.compiler.target>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
    </properties>


    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-ai-bom</artifactId>
                <version>1.0.0</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>


    <dependencies>
<!--        对接智谱-->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-model-zhipuai</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-vector-store</artifactId>

        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-rag</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-advisors-vector-store</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>mysql</groupId>
            <artifactId>mysql-connector-java</artifactId>
            <version>8.0.30</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
            <version>3.4.5</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-model-chat-memory-repository-jdbc</artifactId>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

</project>

**Steps to reproduce**
尝试注入
```
    @Bean
    public MessageChatMemoryAdvisor  messageChatMemory(ChatMemory chatMemory) {

       return  MessageChatMemoryAdvisor.builder(chatMemory)
               .conversationId("1")
                .build();
    }

    @Bean
    public PromptChatMemoryAdvisor promptChatMemoryAdvisor(ChatMemory chatMemory){
        return PromptChatMemoryAdvisor.builder(chatMemory)
                .conversationId("2")
                .build();
    }
```

comments.body: Hi @wenzhuo4657, You can use Mariadb schema for Mysql. Do you see any issues when using `classpath:org/springframework/ai/chat/memory/repository/jdbc/schema-mariadb.sql` ?

The MariaDB schema doesn't work as is for MySQL as the latter doesn't support `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`.

I've opened the PR #3320 for this.
@wenzhuo4657 This issue has been addressed via @hpoettker 's PR. Please re-open if you still see any problem.

> [@hpoettker](https://github.com/hpoettker) Thanks for the PR!
What does PR mean?
